Round 3 (fourth agent dispatch): orchestrator personally caught the round-3 stress test (160g/80k) genuinely DEADLOCKED for 40+ minutes (goroutine dump: scratchpad/goroutine_dump_deadlock.log) -- multiple goroutines permanently blocked on sync.Mutex.Lock inside NodeStore.Lock, called from Tree.findParent/Tree.propagate, each waiting on a latch held by another blocked goroutine: a genuine lock-ordering CYCLE, not just a data race, and very likely the same underlying defect behind 024-verification's unconfirmed silent-data-loss symptom. Root cause confirmed: crabInsert/findParent's hand-over-hand ('lock next, release current') descent used a blocking Lock for every step; because this tree's design lets a node be reached both via its parent's already-linked Children entry AND via a not-yet-linked sibling's NextLeaf/NextSibling pointer at the same time, two concurrent crabbing walks could end up each holding a latch the other needed. Structural fix: every hand-over-hand step in crabInsert/findParent now uses NodeStore.TryLock (new method, latch.go); on a miss, the walk releases everything it holds and restarts from the root (errRestartFromRoot, insert.go) after a small jittered backoff, making a lock-ordering cycle structurally impossible by construction (no goroutine can ever block on a latch while holding a different one). Added TestCrabbingConcurrentPropagateNoDeadlock (insert_test.go): a fast (<0.1s), deterministic regression test using a synchronization hook (crabRetryHook), in the same spirit as engine/mvcc's hook-based pause pattern, to force the exact TryLock-collision interleaving without relying on large-scale random contention. Re-ran the 160g/80k TestCrabbingInsert suite plus the new regression test at -count=10 -race -timeout 5m (165s, all pass, no hang) and the full engine module at -race -timeout 5m (all pass). Also confirmed (again) that round 2's sort.Search fix was a no-op equivalent to the pre-fix 'pos := j' formula; reverted to the simpler form. Cleaned up all round-3 temporary diagnostics (debugWriteHook, zzrepro_instrumented_test.go); kept the O(1) promoted-key bounds check in propagate as a permanent invariant. Superseded the abandoned 2026-07-05/025-implementation-fix run (cut off mid-experiment, never reached implementation) with 2026-07-06/001-implementation-fix. Does NOT independently re-confirm the original silent-data-loss symptom is fixed by this specific change (plausible given the shared mechanism, but not separately proven) -- flagging for /cdr:verify. VERIFIED (2026-07-06-002-verification, 4th pass): deadlock structurally eliminated (30 additional clean repro runs at 160g/80k), silent-data-loss symptom not reproduced in 30 own runs (favorable, not airtight). Fixed EdgeLog.ReadNodeAfter/TruncateNode lock-ordering race vs concurrent AppendEdge: added per-node *sync.Mutex (nodeLocksMu/nodeLocks/LockNode), Compact acquires a node's lock before ReadNodeAfter and holds it through that node's TruncateNode (heldNodeLocks/releaseHeldLocks), AppendEdge holds the same lock for its whole body including fsync. Verifier independently traced lock acquisition/release (no gap), confirmed AppendEdge lock parity, confirmed per-node (not global) granularity via adversarial probe, and found Compact genuinely holds MULTIPLE nodes' locks simultaneously across one pass -- safe only because os.ReadDir's sorted order gives concurrent Compact calls the same acquisition order (verified via 50-node/2-concurrent-Compact stress probe, 5x, no deadlock) but this property is undocumented. 30/30 race-test reps pass, zero flakiness. 2nd of 3 subtasks under issue #49. Non-blocking: (a) undocumented multi-node-simultaneous-lock-holding deadlock-safety property, (b) no permanent test for concurrent Compact calls, (c) carried-forward interleaved multi-node truncate/append/compact test still missing. Issue #38 subtask 4.5.1.3: bounded NodeStore.latches (engine/btree/latch.go) with a refs-counted eviction scheme (acquireLatch/releaseLatch wrapping Lock/Unlock/TryLock/Version), evicting an entry only when refs hits 0 AND version is still pristine 0 (never-written nodes), to prevent Tree.Lookup's optimistic-read protocol from silently missing a concurrent write via a version-counter reset. Public Lock/Unlock/TryLock/Version signatures unchanged; diff confined to latch.go + latch_test.go. Verifier independently traced acquireLatch/releaseLatch/peekLatch/Lock/Unlock/TryLock/Version line by line and confirmed via mutation testing that the version==0 gate and TryLock-failure reference release both genuinely work and are non-vacuously tested (removing either causes the corresponding new test to fail). Independently re-derived and confirmed the commitLocks-vs-EpochManager.refcounts discrepancy claim from engine/mvcc source directly. Full engine/btree (-race -v -count=5) and engine/... (-race -count=2) suites pass except the pre-existing unrelated engine/split flake. BLOCKING FINDING: TestNodeLatchNoDoubleLockAcrossEviction, built specifically to guard Unlock's mutex-unlock-before-refcount-release ordering (documented in-code as unconditionally 'load-bearing'), does not detect a deliberate reversal of that ordering even under heavy additional stress testing (200 goroutines x 20000 iters/goroutine, GOMAXPROCS=16, -race, x3). Root-cause analysis shows the current code is not actually exploitable (version==0 gate + content-before-Unlock invariant provide redundant protection), so no live production bug was found, but the claimed invariant is unproven by any test on the single riskiest concurrency primitive touched in issue #38 so far. Required before merge/close: add a deterministic hook-based test proving the ordering claim, or correct the doc comment to accurately scope it as defense-in-depth.

title: Bound engine/split/guard.go's FileGuard registry with eviction (fix-cycle 2 of 3)
notes: Resolves 070-verification's CHANGES_REQUESTED (Release's claimed load-bearing Store(false)-before-releaseGuard ordering unproven). Fix-cycle chose to disprove (not merely leave unproven) the original doc claim: corrected guard.go's doc comment to state the real, verifier-confirmed behavior (reversing the order breaks eviction *progress*, not correctness -- FileGuard's eviction gate is refs==0 && !inProgress.Load(), and inProgress is the exact flag Release clears), and added a deterministic regression test proving both halves. Independently re-verified at 076-verification: PASS. Known commit-hygiene caveat: commit ce62682 also unintentionally swept in engine/split/orchestrate.go/orchestrate_test.go (a concurrent agent's unrelated subtask 4.5.3.3 work) due to shared-git-index contamination under concurrency, self-disclosed in 072-implementation's own handoff.json commit_scope_incident field -- content itself is accurate and harmless, matches the established 'concurrent-agent working-tree bleed' pattern already documented elsewhere in pending.md (issue #55 4.5.17.4/4.5.17.2). Retroactively added by task #21's milestone #10 .cdr/ audit pass -- this entry was missing from task.jsonl entirely despite existing, completed run artifacts.

title: Add automatic recovery/timeout for abandoned SPLITTING catalog records
notes: Lease/heartbeat-based reclaimIfExpired mechanism added to engine/split/orchestrate.go; its own content landed as part of commit ce62682 (a concurrent agent's unrelated 4.5.3.2 commit -- confirmed via `git diff HEAD -- engine/split/orchestrate.go engine/split/orchestrate_test.go` being empty at that commit, per 074-implementation's own commit_note) rather than its own dedicated commit, due to shared-git-index contamination under concurrency. BLOCKING finding at 077-verification: reclaimIfExpired's guard reclamation is not fenced against a still-alive-but-slow legitimate holder -- FileGuard.Release(fileID) is unconditional/tokenless, so a lease-timeout misjudgment can hand the guard to a second concurrent BeginSplit caller while the original holder is still actively executing its split, producing two simultaneous 'exclusive' split holders for the same fileID. Retroactively added by task #21's milestone #10 .cdr/ audit pass -- this entry, and its fix-cycle-2 entry below, were missing from task.jsonl entirely despite existing, completed run artifacts.

title: Add automatic recovery/timeout for abandoned SPLITTING catalog records (fix-cycle 2 of 3)
commit_note: Fix-cycle spans two commits reviewed together at 088-verification: 78a18180bf6e611b212a9ba4cba29af0904c1f5f and 15bfde5.
notes: Resolves 077-verification's CHANGES_REQUESTED (reclaimIfExpired could release a still-live holder's FileGuard, enabling genuine concurrent double-split execution). Fix: reclaimIfExpired no longer calls guard.Release at all; BeginSplit no longer retries TryAcquire after a reclaim; Orchestrator.mu is held across both reclaimIfExpired's and EndSplit's transitionStatus calls, eliminating the TOCTOU window between them for the same fileID. Traced by hand and confirmed by a real-goroutine concurrency test (TestReclaimNeverDoubleAcquiresGuardForSlowHolder) independently re-verified at 088-verification: PASS_WITH_COMMENTS. Retroactively added by task #21's milestone #10 .cdr/ audit pass.
